intro: Forget the red flags — what's the green flag your relationship is built on? Answer 8 questions to find your couple's biggest strength.

questions.text: The best thing about how you two argue is…

options.label: Neither of us threatens to leave
I'm never afraid to be honest
We both try to do better after

questions.text: When something's bothering you, you can…

options.label: Say it plainly and be heard
Trust they won't hold it against you
Joke about it to break the ice
Turn it into a lesson together

questions.text: Around your partner, you feel free to…

options.label: Have a whole life of your own too
Spend time with your own friends
Give you space if you need it
Make you laugh till it's better
Let you just be, no fixing
Remind you of the bigger picture

questions.text: The thing friends envy most about you two is…

options.label: How you push each other forward

questions.text: When you mess up, your partner…

options.label: Teases you, then lets it go

communication.title: Communication
communication.description: Your biggest green flag is that you actually talk. Hard things get said, feelings get heard, and nothing festers in silence. That's far rarer than it should be.
communication.tip: Keep asking the question most couples skip: 'how are we doing, really?'

trust.title: Trust
trust.description: Your relationship runs on rock-solid trust. No games, no score-keeping, no threats to walk away — just two people who feel genuinely secure.
trust.tip: Trust grows on kept promises; keep showing up in the small things.

playfulness.title: Playfulness
playfulness.description: Your superpower is fun. You laugh through the hard stuff, tease without stinging, and keep things light even when life isn't.
playfulness.tip: Leave room for the occasional serious talk too — play and depth aren't enemies.

safety.title: Emotional Safety
safety.description: Your biggest green flag is safety. You can be a mess, set a boundary, or cry — and you'll be met with kindness every single time.
safety.tip: Protect this fiercely; emotional safety is the soil everything else grows in.

growth.title: Growth
growth.description: You make each other better. You celebrate each other's wins, push each other forward, and turn mistakes into lessons instead of grudges.
growth.tip: Make sure you also accept each other as-is; growth lands best on a bed of acceptance.
